name: DeafBlind Ontario Services
address: 17665 Leslie St, Unit 15 Newmarket, ON, L3Y 3E3 (387km) Clipboard
description:
DeafBlind Ontario Services is such an amazing organization filling an often overlooked group of Ontarian's; those who are deaf/blind.

When I visited their supportive living homes and meeting the resident's and their intervenor's who support them, I was blown away.  The care, attention, and dignity shown to each person was heartwarming, and the specialized housing, equipment, and routines provide residents the opportunity to live on their own with assistance.

Such amazing individual stories, this is a worthwhile charity that you should definitely consider supporting on a regular basis so they can continue to expand their services in Ontario.
Thank you Deaf Blind Ontario for all you do.

My son has been living in one of the residences since he turned 21. I do not know what we would have done without the help of this fabulous organization, DeafBlind Ontario Services. The people in the organization but especially the Intervenors are just amazing and so caring. The programs are geared to each individuals needs. The first time my son was home for a visit and after only 2 days brought me his suitcase, was a hard thing to accept but it made me realize I did not have to worry as  much anymore, he was happy there and he wanted to go back to his HOME and Friends.

Deafblind Ontario Services is unique in the amazing support they provide our daughter and are able to adapt to her complex and evolving needs.
We have gained great peace of mind knowing that she is so well supported by an organization that provides excellent over sight from the top down and engages only the very best intervenors who are dedicated, highly skilled, caring and devoted to ensuring our daughter is given every  opportunity to develop a rich and fulfilling life.
We are truly grateful for DeafBlind Ontario Services and the huge difference they have made, not only in our daughter’s life, but all of those who love her! I do not know what we would have done without this incredible organization!!!

Our daughter has been living in a residence run by DeafBlind Ontario Services (DBOS) for the last 7 years.  We have been very happy with the decision as it has given our daughter a rich and fun adult life in a home-like, caring environment.  DBOS is a professional well-run not-for -profit organization with leaders and staff that are passionate about providing a safe nurturing environment for their clients.

My son lives in one of the residences and I honestly don't know what I would have done without this wonderful organization.
The intervenors provide wonderful care for my son who also has physical disabilities.  With the help of these amazing and caring intervenors, he is able to participate in activities and outings which gives him the opportunity to enjoy different experiences.

Thank you DeafBliind Ontario and Thank you to all the intervenors.

name: Karis Disability Services
address: 26 Peppler St Kitchener, ON, N2J 3C4 (492km) Clipboard
description:
Attending this family camp was truly life-changing for our family. From the moment we arrived, we felt an incredible level of support, understanding, and acceptance. As a large family of children with disabilities, it’s rare to find a space where everyone feels seen, supported, and included and this camp provided exactly that. For the first time in a long time, we felt truly whole. We were able to relax, breathe, and just be a family without the usual stress or worry. The environment allowed us to reconnect with one another, strengthen our bond as a family, and grow closer to God. This experience gave us rest for our minds, hearts, and souls. We are so grateful for the love, care, and community we found here, and we will cherish these memories forever.

address: 3939 Duke of York Blvd, Unit 403 Mississauga, ON, L5B 4N2 (14km) Clipboard
description:
Connect 4 Life is a warm and welcoming place that supports people in building confidence and life skills. The staff are very kind, patient, and understanding, and they always make sure everyone feels comfortable and included. The programs are helpful, easy to follow, and focused on improving communication, social skills, and independence. Overall, it is a positive environment where people learn, grow, and feel respected.

Connect4Life is an amazing organization that truly supports people with disabilities.
Their programs are helpful, practical, and very empowering.
I really appreciate their positive impact on the community.
Highly recommend their services!

Transformative Experience!
The Connect 4 Wellness Program with Connect 4 Life has been a game-changer for my overall well-being. From the start, the team was incredibly supportive, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in helping me grow—physically, mentally, emotionally, and spiritually. The holistic approach they take really sets this program apart. I’ve gained tools and insights that have not only improved my health but also brought more balance and purpose to my life. I can’t recommend this program enough to anyone looking to make lasting, positive change. Thank you, Connect 4 Life!

Connect 4 Life offers amazing, accessible programs that empower people with disabilities. CEO Melanie Taddeo is passionate and dedicated to helping others achieve their dreams. Highly recommend for anyone looking for inclusive opportunities and meaningful support!

name: Providence Care
address: 525 Montreal St Kingston, ON, K7K 3H9 (8km) Clipboard
description:
I've never experienced the type of compassion and dedication to truely help people from any medical professional, until I started seeing a doctor at Providence care. After attempting to get ODSP for almost a decade, I found most health care professionals are not willing to give a diagnosis for mental health or mood disorders. They'll tell you "we don't like to put labels on people". But the truth is they are pressured by the provincial government to be as unhelpful as possible as Doug Ford doesn't believe in mental health or social assistance programs. Not only did I get a diagnosis, but actual dedicated and ongoing assistance both with my ODSP application and treatment. I am so thankful to Dr. Milev and the wonderful staff here. I'll never forget what you've all done for me.

What rude receptionists and service! I call to inquire about a family member there, and was very rudely told no visitors and she hung up. Wow.. really feeling like that's a place that cares about people.
*Update*
So after receiving a blanket covered generated response to my review, I make the effort to call the number they provide.  To which it rings and rings and no one answers. Again, really making the "care system" seem like they care.
Thank you. I will make sure to never refer any other family members or friends here

Attended this hospital a few weeks ago for a consultation. Asked volunteers at the front doors where to go for registration and the tone given to me made it seem like I was inconveniencing them. The person at registration was helpful, however.

The person I consulted with ended the appointment early, told me to follow up, no secretary so left a message and never received a call back after leaving a message.

Unfortunate because I was hoping that the services offered by this hospital would help me. Instead, it seems that this hospital is no better than Kingston General, which is to say, it sucks.

Thank you for leaving me demoralized and not interested in pursing any further help from the system.

I've repeatedly asked for a referral here and have been denied by clinics and hospitals. Mental health care is inaccessible and to the detriment of my health and others'.

It’s a beautiful hospital and the physio for my parent was top notch. He was an extremely rare case with limited data on how he can expect to recover in the future so I don’t hold that against anyone. Our only complaints are 1) He was sent home before our house was renovated to accommodate a person with disabilities. We were promised he wouldn’t leave until we were equipped for it, but that was not the case.

2) An occupational therapist put down the wrong measurements for his wheelchair, so the rental one we received was too big for any doors in our house, we had to take doors off and the chair is also way too big for him as a person.

3) He requires a mechanical lift to get in and out of his chair, a nurse dropped him at PC from a ceiling lift and he is still dealing with pain from that and this was months ago.

4) I’m not sure if she was a nurse or PSW, but one asked him if he had a brain injury because he didn’t know the answer to one of her questions. He doesn’t, but super inappropriate question to ask in a place like PC where they deal with brain injuries constantly. The nursing manager did absolutely nothing about this and was upset with my parent for calling her a name after that. What’s worse to you?

No complaints on the actual establishment, just some of the staff should not be there or need a huge wake up call. A good percentage of these patients are recovering from life altering injuries, take that into consideration.

I strongly caution Black women and other minorities to seek support elsewhere to protect their mental health.

name: Community Living Elgin
address: 7 Morrison Dr St Thomas, ON, N5R 4S5 (25km) Clipboard
description:
Community Living Elgin in St. Thomas is one of the best individual private care and disability care centers in the area. With over 60 years of dedicated service. They truly set the standard for excellence in supporting individuals with developmental disabilities. The commitment of their team to empowering each person to live a fulfilling, independent, and meaningful life is evident in everything they do.

With a highly dedicated and compassionate staff, they offer a broad range of quality services — including supported living, skill development, recreational programs, and community integration — all tailored to the unique goals and abilities of each individual. Every program is carefully personalized, ensuring dignity, respect, and a true sense of belonging for everyone they support.

Community Living Elgin stands out as a trusted and invaluable resource for families in St. Thomas and surrounding areas. It’s a safe, welcoming, and professional environment where care, inclusion, and individual growth truly matter. Without question, they are one of the top choices for personalized disability and private care services in Ontario.

description:
I had a wonderful experience overall with this place. From the moment I walked in, the staff were welcoming, professional, and genuine. They took the time to understand my needs after my accident and designed a treatment plan that actually felt effective and personalized.

The entire atmosphere is positive and encouraging, and you can tell that everyone there truly cares about their clients recovery and well being. The clinic is easy to access on Bank Street. I really appreciated how smoothly everything was handled, from scheduling appointments to following up after sessions.

I’d highly recommend this place to anyone who is looking for professional, compassionate, and result driven rehabilitation care, especially if you are not well versed in the world of car accidents, the forms and communication can get confusing so it was nice to have things explained.
